# UC-11.5.4 · Webex Device Health

## Description

Room devices with high CPU, thermal, or firmware errors degrade meetings; proactive health reduces onsite truck rolls and VIP room incidents.

## Value

Room devices with high CPU, thermal, or firmware errors degrade meetings; proactive health reduces onsite truck rolls and VIP room incidents.

## Implementation

Ingest Control Hub device inventory and health APIs. Poll or stream alerts for offline, warning, or error states. Track firmware version drift. Alert on sustained high CPU or temperature before automatic thermal throttling.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `ta_cisco_webex_add_on_for_splunk`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=webex:device`, Webex Control Hub device telemetry.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest Control Hub device inventory and health APIs. Poll or stream alerts for offline, warning, or error states. Track firmware version drift. Alert on sustained high CPU or temperature before automatic thermal throttling.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=webex sourcetype="webex:device"
| where health_state!="ok" OR cpu_pct > 85 OR temperature_c > 45
| stats latest(health_state) as state, max(cpu_pct) as max_cpu, max(temperature_c) as max_temp by device_id, product
| sort -max_cpu
```

Understanding this SPL

**Webex Device Health** — Room devices with high CPU, thermal, or firmware errors degrade meetings; proactive health reduces onsite truck rolls and VIP room incidents.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=webex:device`, Webex Control Hub device telemetry. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `ta_cisco_webex_add_on_for_splunk`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: webex; **sourcetype**: webex:device. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=webex, sourcetype="webex:device". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where health_state!="ok" OR cpu_pct > 85 OR temperature_c > 45` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by device_id, product** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Webex Control Hub device and meeting analytics for the same room or user.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (device × health), Table (devices over threshold), Line chart (CPU/temperature trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=webex sourcetype="webex:device"
| where health_state!="ok" OR cpu_pct > 85 OR temperature_c > 45
| stats latest(health_state) as state, max(cpu_pct) as max_cpu, max(temperature_c) as max_temp by device_id, product
| sort -max_cpu
```

## Visualization

Status grid (device × health), Table (devices over threshold), Line chart (CPU/temperature trend).
